name: agent_negotiation
description: a two-agent negotiation world over a deal; actions offer, counter, accept, walk_away

card.description: a two-agent negotiation world over a deal; actions offer, counter, accept, walk_away

rules: Two agents, 'buyer' and 'seller', negotiate over a single deal price.
Only the agent whose turn it is acts; the turn alternates after every offer or counter.
'offer' and 'counter' place a price on the table and pass the turn to the other agent; 'counter' is only valid once an offer already exists.
'accept' closes the deal at the current offer, but only the agent who did NOT make that offer may accept it.
'walk_away' ends the negotiation immediately with no deal.
Once the status is 'deal' or 'no_deal' the negotiation is frozen and no action changes it.
A mutually beneficial deal is only possible when seller_reservation <= buyer_reservation.

transition.code:
def _default_price(s, actor):
    # A deterministic concession used when an action carries no explicit price,
    # so rollouts stay replayable. Buyers open low, sellers open high, and each
    # subsequent move concedes halfway toward the midpoint of the bargaining zone.
    lo = float(s.get("seller_reservation", 0))
    hi = float(s.get("buyer_reservation", 0))
    mid = (lo + hi) / 2.0
    cur = s.get("current_offer")
    if cur is None:
        return lo if actor == "buyer" else hi
    cur = float(cur)
    return round(cur + (mid - cur) / 2.0, 2)


def transition(state, action):
    s = dict(state)
    name = action.get("name")
    params = action.get("params") or {}
    agent = action.get("agent")

    # A closed negotiation is frozen: no action changes a settled outcome.
    if s.get("status") != "open":
        return s

    # The acting agent: an explicit buyer/seller, else whoever holds the turn.
    actor = agent if agent in ("buyer", "seller") else s.get("turn")
    other = "seller" if actor == "buyer" else "buyer"

    if name in ("offer", "counter"):
        # A counter only makes sense once a price is already on the table.
        if name == "counter" and s.get("current_offer") is None:
            return s
        price = params.get("price")
        if price is None:
            price = _default_price(s, actor)
        s["current_offer"] = round(float(price), 2)
        s["offer_by"] = actor
        s["turn"] = other
        s["round"] = int(s.get("round", 0)) + 1
        s["last_action"] = name
        s["last_actor"] = actor

    elif name == "accept":
        # You can only accept an offer the OTHER party put on the table.
        if s.get("current_offer") is None or s.get("offer_by") == actor:
            return s
        s["status"] = "deal"
        s["agreed_price"] = s["current_offer"]
        s["turn"] = None
        s["last_action"] = "accept"
        s["last_actor"] = actor

    elif name == "walk_away":
        # Either party may abandon the table at any point, ending in no deal.
        s["status"] = "no_deal"
        s["turn"] = None
        s["last_action"] = "walk_away"
        s["last_actor"] = actor

    return s

emit.report: Negotiation status: {status} (round {round}). Current offer: {current_offer} by {offer_by}; next to act: {turn}. Agreed price: {agreed_price}.
